advanced/architecture.title: Architecture
advanced/architecture.description: The guard package is organized into five layers, each with a distinct responsibility. Dependencies flow strictly downward -- enforcement depends on evaluation, evaluation depends on policy, and policy depends on tokens.

advanced/gxp.title: GxP Infrastructure
advanced/gxp.description: For regulated environments (pharmaceutical, biotech, medical devices), the guard package includes infrastructure that supports audit trail requirements, data integrity, and system lifecycle management.

api/api-reference.title: API Reference
api/api-reference.description: Complete reference for all exports from @hex-di/guard.

concepts/evaluation.title: Evaluation
concepts/evaluation.description: The evaluation layer is the pure core of the guard system. It takes a policy and a subject, walks the policy tree, and produces a Decision with a full trace.

concepts/permissions.title: Permissions
concepts/permissions.description: Permissions are the atomic units of authorization. Each permission is a branded nominal token -- a value that carries its identity at both the type level and runtime.

concepts/policies.title: Policies & Composition
concepts/policies.description: Policies are serializable discriminated unions composed through algebraic combinators. Every policy is plain JSON data -- no callbacks, no closures, no hidden state.

concepts/roles.title: Roles & Inheritance
concepts/roles.description: Roles aggregate permissions into named sets and support DAG-based inheritance with automatic permission flattening.

concepts/subjects.title: Subjects
concepts/subjects.description: The AuthSubject is the identity being evaluated against a policy. It carries the subject's permissions, roles, and attributes.

guides/di-integration.title: DI Integration
guides/di-integration.description: The enforcement layer bridges the pure evaluation core with HexDI's runtime container. enforcePolicy() wraps adapters so that policy evaluation runs automatically at resolution time.

guides/port-gates.title: Port Gates
guides/port-gates.description: Port gate hooks provide infrastructure-level authorization at the container level. They operate before subject-aware guard enforcement and are designed for coarse-grained access control.

guides/serialization.title: Serialization
guides/serialization.description: Policies in @hex-di/guard are data, not callbacks. This is a deliberate design choice that enables serialization, storage, transmission, and introspection.

index.title: Introduction
index.description: A policy-based authorization library for TypeScript applications, featuring composable policies, audit trails, and deep integration with dependency injection.

react.title: React Integration
react.description: The @hex-di/guard-react package provides React components and hooks for integrating authorization into your UI, with full support for React Suspense.

testing.title: Testing
testing.description: The @hex-di/guard-testing package provides comprehensive testing utilities including memory adapters, fixtures, custom matchers, and conformance suites.
